blocks.headline: Get EHCP Assessments That Meet Your Statutory Deadlines
blocks.subtitle: HCPC-registered EPs delivering evidence-based EHCP assessments within 16 weeks. Clear recommendations, practical guidance, statutory compliance guaranteed.

blocks.heading: What is an EHCP Assessment and Why Do You Need One?

children.text: An EHCP is a statutory document describing a child's special educational needs and required support. To create an EHCP, the local authority gathers evidence from multiple professionals, including an Educational Psychologist.
The EP's role is to:
Assess the child's cognitive abilities, learning style, and specific needs
Identify strengths and barriers to learning
Recommend evidence-based support and interventions
Provide clarity on the type and level of support needed
A quality EHCP assessment report gives the LA and schools the foundation to design effective support and allocate resources.
Key Timescales
Local authority has 6 weeks from referral to decide whether to assess
If assessment proceeds, LA has 10 weeks for evidence gathering (including the EP assessment)
LA has 8 weeks to write and issue the final EHCP
Total: 16 weeks from referral to issued plan
(or decision not to assess)
Psychology Direct understands these timelines and plans assessments accordingly.

blocks.heading: What We Provide for EHCP Assessments
blocks.subheading: A complete assessment service, from initial enquiry to panel-ready report.

cards.title: Initial Consultation
cards.description: Free consultation with an experienced EP to understand the child's needs and confirm assessment is appropriate. Helps you prepare for the formal assessment.

cards.title: Comprehensive Assessment
cards.description: 2–4 hours covering cognitive ability, attainment, learning style, social-emotional development, and functional impact. Multiple methods: testing, observation, interviews.

cards.title: Evidence-Based Report
cards.description: Detailed report addressing all statutory questions. Clear diagnosis, practical recommendations, actionable strategies for support. Meets SEND Code of Practice standards.

cards.title: 100% Quality Assurance
cards.description: Every report is quality-checked before delivery. Verified for statutory compliance, completeness, and clarity.

cards.title: Practical Recommendations
cards.description: We provide specific, actionable recommendations for school support, specialist input, and intervention strategies.

cards.title: Post-Assessment Support
cards.description: After report delivery, the EP can brief the school, attend EHCP meetings, or provide follow-up consultation.

children.text: Preparation is key. Before the EP arrives:
Schools and parents should gather initial observations (how does the child struggle?)
Previous assessments, reports, or diagnoses should be available
Key people involved (teachers, parents, other professionals) should be ready to speak with the EP
The referral form provided by the LA should be completed fully
Our referral form gathers this information in advance, so the EP arrives fully briefed. Less time wasted getting up to speed, more time focused on assessment.
A typical EHCP assessment includes:
Interview with parent/carer (background, development, home context)
Interview with teacher/SENCO (school presentation, observed difficulties, strategies tried)
Direct assessment: cognitive testing, attainment testing, observation, functional assessment
Review of previous assessments or diagnoses
Structured interviews exploring emotional, social, and behavioural factors
The EP will be warm, professional, and sensitive to the child's experience. Assessment may be split across multiple visits as needed.
Once the EP completes the assessment, we:
Analyse findings and write the report (typically 2 weeks)
Quality-check the report to ensure all statutory questions are answered
Deliver the report to you (school and/or LA)
Offer to brief the school if helpful
Make the EP available for EHCP panel meetings or follow-up if needed
The report is written for the LA, schools, parents, and other professionals. Language is clear, not jargony.

tabs.label: Statutory Questions the Assessment Must Address

children.text: LA officers will ask the EP about:
The child's cognitive ability and attainment
Specific learning difficulties or disabilities
Social, emotional, and mental health needs
Communication and interaction abilities
Sensory or physical needs
How these needs affect the child's learning
What support would help the child thrive
A quality assessment report answers all these questions with evidence.

blocks.heading: Frequently Asked Questions About EHCP Assessments

faqs.question: What is an EHCP assessment?
faqs.answer: An EHCP assessment is a specialist psychological evaluation to determine if a child needs an Education, Health and Care Plan. An Educational Psychologist gathers evidence about the child's cognitive ability, learning style, needs, and how to support them. The assessment report helps the local authority decide what support the child requires and at what level.

faqs.question: How long does an EHCP assessment take?
faqs.answer: The actual assessment typically takes 2–4 hours, spread across one or more visits. The EP's analysis and report takes around 2 weeks. From referral to issued EHCP, the statutory timeline is 16 weeks (6 weeks decision, 10 weeks evidence gathering, 8 weeks plan writing).

faqs.question: How much does an EHCP assessment cost?
faqs.answer: Costs vary depending on assessment complexity and your location. We always provide a fixed quotation upfront. For local authorities, we offer volume discounts. For schools, costs are typically £800–1,500. Contact us for a specific quote.

faqs.question: Can you meet the 16-week statutory timeline?
faqs.answer: Yes. We understand statutory EHCP timelines and prioritise assessments accordingly. Standard turnaround from assessment to report is 2 weeks, which fits comfortably within LA timeframes. For urgent cases, faster turnaround is available.

faqs.question: What will the EHCP assessment report include?
faqs.answer: A comprehensive report covering cognitive assessment, attainment testing, observations of learning and behaviour, summary of needs, diagnosis or disability clarification, recommendations for support and interventions, and practical guidance for schools. Reports are evidence-based and meet SEND Code of Practice standards.

faqs.question: Will my local authority accept an EHCP report from Psychology Direct?
faqs.answer: Yes. All our EPs are HCPC-registered and experienced in LA EHCP assessments. We work to your LA's template or our own format. We provide evidence of our vetting and quality assurance.

faqs.question: What if the child has already been assessed by someone else?
faqs.answer: If there's a recent assessment (within 1–2 years) from a qualified professional, it may be sufficient. However, an updated EHCP-focused assessment is often needed to address specific statutory questions. Contact us to discuss whether reassessment is necessary.

faqs.question: Can the assessment cover specific learning difficulties like dyslexia or autism?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our EPs are experienced in assessing dyslexia, dyscalculia, ADHD, autism, attachment difficulties, and other specific learning needs. The EHCP assessment will clarify whether these difficulties are present and how they affect the child's learning.

faqs.question: What if the child is very young (under 5)?
faqs.answer: EHCP assessments can begin from age 0, though they're more common from school age onwards. Very young child assessments are different (play-based observation, parent interviews, developmental screening). Discuss your child's age and needs when you contact us.

faqs.question: What happens if the EHCP panel rejects the recommendations in the report?
faqs.answer: This is rare. Quality EHCP assessment reports are evidence-based and rarely challenged. If a panel has questions, the EP can explain or clarify. We're available for panel meetings if needed.

faqs.question: Can the EP attend the EHCP panel meeting?
faqs.answer: Yes. In some cases, the school or LA may invite the EP to present findings or answer questions. We can provide this support if it's helpful. Discuss this option when you book the assessment.

faqs.question: What happens after the EHCP is issued? Can the EP still support the child?
faqs.answer: Yes. After the plan is issued, the EP can provide ongoing consultation, strategy reviews, progress monitoring, or advice to the school as the child's needs evolve. The assessment doesn't have to end once the plan is issued.

blocks.heading: Ready to Commission an EHCP Assessment?
blocks.bodyText: Whether you're a school needing an urgent assessment or an LA clearing a backlog, we'll match you with an experienced EP and deliver a statutory-compliant report on time. Let's get started.
